symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic shallow medial-malleolar ulcer, dull-aching, relieved by elevation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic venous leg ulcer with acute secondary cellulitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Medial-malleolar (gaiter-area) ulcer relieved by elevation is the classic venous ulcer pattern.
Arterial ulcers are painful on elevation and typically distal/pressure-point, not relieved by raising the leg.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute increase in thick, foul-smelling purulent exudate soaking dressings

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sudden rise in purulent malodorous exudate signals bacterial infection of the wound bed.

symptoms.symptom_name: New sharp throbbing pain, worse on standing, distinct from the old dull ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A change to new throbbing pain marks an acute inflammatory/infective flare on the chronic wound.
Neuropathic ulcers are characteristically painless; significant pain argues against a purely neuropathic aetiology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender, warm, erythematous 2-3 cm rim of skin around the ulcer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Expanding tender warm erythema of the surrounding skin is the defining clinical sign of cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, rigors or systemic upset (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, rigors and tachycardia points to a localised rather than systemic infection, supporting oral outpatient antibiotics over IV/hospital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic lipodermatosclerosis, haemosiderin (brown) staining and mild ankle oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic venous insufficiency / venous leg ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lipodermatosclerosis and haemosiderin staining are hallmark chronic venous skin changes; visible-on-video and patient-describable in broad terms.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved, palpable distal pedal pulses (dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal distal pulses make significant peripheral arterial disease unlikely and support safe compression once infection settles — but pulse palpation and ABPI require in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with normal vital signs on clinician measurement (Temp 37.3, HR 88, BP 145/88, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objectively normal vitals confirm the patient is systemically well — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers 'systemically well' from reported absence of fever/rigors, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wound swab microscopy, culture and sensitivities

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A swab can guide antibiotic choice if the wound fails to respond, but empirical antibiotics should not wait for it; never obtainable over telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute infective flare on a chronic venous ulcer
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Chronic venous leg ulcer with acute secondary cellulitis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The triad of new purulent malodorous exudate, new throbbing pain and an expanding tender warm erythematous rim on a chronic venous ulcer is the clinical signature of a secondarily-infected venous ulcer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ankle-brachial pressure index (ABPI) and pedal pulse assessment before compression
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Arterial supply must be confirmed adequate before compression therapy is (re)started; requires in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guides targeted antibiotics if the wound fails to respond to empirical therapy; does not delay starting antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine venous duplex ultrasound during the acute infection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in the acute infective phase; venous assessment belongs to later chronic-ulcer management, not this urgent contact.
